question: What's the best time to visit Ulladulla NSW with kids?
answer: Spring (September-November) and autumn (March-May) are absolute pearlers for visiting Ulladulla with little ones! You'll get gorgeous weather without the summer crowds, and accommodation prices are more wallet-friendly. That said, summer's brilliant for beach days if you don't mind the hustle and bustle. Winter's quieter and great for whale watching, though the water's a bit nippy for swimming. School holidays book out quickly year-round, so get in early!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Ulladulla NSW?
answer: Good news – several holiday parks around Ulladulla welcome your furry family members! Many parks have designated pet-friendly cabins or powered sites where well-behaved dogs are allowed. You'll typically need to keep them on-lead and clean up after them. It's worth ringing ahead to confirm their pet policy and any additional fees. Some parks have restrictions during peak season, so always check before booking to avoid disappointment!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Ulladulla NSW during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay around $150-$300 per night for a basic cabin sleeping 4-6 people, with fancier options up to $400+. Powered caravan sites usually run $50-$80 per night. Prices jump significantly during Christmas and Easter, and most parks have minimum stay requirements (usually 3-7 nights). Off-peak rates can be 30-50% cheaper, so there's real savings if you've got flexibility!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Beyond the usual clothes and toiletries, pack beach gear (swimmers, hats, sunscreen, towels), insect repellent, and a basic first aid kit. Bring bikes, scooters, or outdoor toys – holiday parks have heaps of space! Most cabins have kitchen basics, but pack favourite snacks and condiments. Don't forget nightlights, comfort toys, and any special bedding for little ones. A portable high chair's handy if you've got a bub!

question: What is there to do near Ulladulla NSW holiday parks for families?
answer: You're spoilt for choice! The harbour's perfect for fish feeding and spotting pelicans. There are gorgeous calm beaches like Mollymook ideal for young swimmers, plus rockpools to explore. Visit the Funland amusement park, try mini golf, or hire bikes for the coastal track. The weekly farmers market's a hit, and you're close to Pigeon House Mountain for easier family bushwalks. Plenty of playgrounds and BBQ spots too!
